source: official public Sequencing.com and get.sequencing.com bundle pages
privacyBoundary: Public marketing and FAQ text only. No account page, order submission, Start App action, generated result page, raw genome data, private result URL, or personal variant value was captured.
purpose: Record official public bundle-inclusion evidence for current formal-pending packages while preserving the no-promotion boundary for formal report output.

promotionBoundary.reason: These public pages identify bundle contents, report availability, and broad screen scope, but they do not expose package-specific sample rows, completed report rows, output tables, or row-level source bindings.

sources.title: The Most Complete DNA Health Screen - Whole Genome Sequencing
observedFacts: Complete Health Screen WGS Bundle is offered at $399 in the public page copy.
The bundle page lists raw/analyzed data access including FASTQ, BAM, and VCF files covering SNPs, INDELs, SVs, CNVs, and mitochondrial heteroplasmy.

The page lists health areas included: Rare Disease Screening, Carrier Screening, Connective Tissue Disorders and EDS, Autoimmune Disorders, Developmental Disorders, Cardiovascular Health, Brain Health, Medication & Drug Response, Reproductive & Hormonal Health, Cancer Risk, Skin Health, and Neurological Health.
The page describes Next-Gen Disease Screen as analyzing whole genome sequencing data for more than 15,000 diseases, conditions, traits, and medication reactions.

sources.title: FTDNA Exclusive Whole Genome Sequencing Bundle
observedFacts: The page lists Health and Data Whole Genome Sequencing Bundle health reports and advanced analyses including Genome Explorer, Next-Gen Disease Screen, Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome, Carrier Screening, Autoimmune Disorders, Cardiovascular Health, Medication & Drug Response, Connective Tissue Disorders, Brain Health, Reproductive & Hormonal Health, and Cancer Risk.
The FAQ states every Sequencing kit bundle includes 30x whole genome sequencing of all approximately 30,000 genes and all chromosomes end-to-end.
The FAQ states the bundle includes comprehensive analysis of more than 15,000 conditions and medication reactions.
The FAQ states a Summary Report of results is downloadable as PDF and additional AI Reports are downloadable as PDF, but the page does not expose those PDF report rows publicly.
The FAQ says Sequencing.com provides screening for all types of Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome known to be associated with genetic variants and notes hypermobile EDS is limited/preliminary.
